title: Vegetable Pulao
description: A fragrant one-pot rice dish made with basmati rice, aromatic whole spices, and colorful mixed vegetables. Light, flavorful, and perfect for everyday meals or festive occasions.

ingredients.title: For Pulao Base
items: 3 tbsp ghee (clarified butter) or neutral oil
2 green chilies, slit lengthwise (adjust heat as desired)
1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, cauliflower, capsicum) – cut into small cubes

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Rice
steps: Rinse basmati rice gently under running water until the water runs clear. Soak for 20 minutes, then drain.
In a pot, bring 3 cups of water to a boil with bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and salt.
Add soaked rice, cook until 90% done (grains should be just tender but not broken).
Drain and set aside to prevent overcooking.

instruction.title: Step 2: Make the Pulao Base
steps: Heat ghee/oil in a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add cumin seeds, and when they splutter, add bay leaf, sliced onions, and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger–garlic paste, sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add slit green chilies and vegetables; sauté for 3–4 minutes.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, salt, and mix well. Sprinkle a little water and cover; cook until vegetables are just tender.

instruction.title: Step 3: Combine & Steam
steps: Add the pre-cooked rice gently to the vegetable mixture.
Sprinkle garam masala and fresh coriander.
Mix gently so grains remain separate.
Cover with a tight-fitting lid and steam on low heat for 8–10 minutes (dum method).

instruction.title: Step 4: Serve
steps: Garnish with fried cashews and lemon wedges.
Serve hot with raita, papad, or pickle.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Garnish with fried cashews and fresh coriander.

chefTips: Rice texture: Always parboil rice separately for perfect grain separation.
Vegetable choice: Seasonal vegetables enhance aroma and color.
Ghee vs Oil: Ghee adds richness; for vegan pulao, use vegetable oil or coconut oil.
Dum cooking: Wrapping the lid with a kitchen towel prevents steam from escaping, ensuring aromatic infusion.
